content.text: Shared platform libraries need a published support policy just like external open-source packages. Without one, consuming teams have no basis for deciding when to upgrade, and the platform team has no mechanism to retire old versions safely. This sub-subtopic defines the versioning scheme, support windows, deprecation process, release cadence, security response SLA, migration guide format, pre-release testing, Python compatibility matrix, and enforcement strategy used for the

All platform libraries follow Semantic Versioning 2.0 (semver). The rules below specify exactly what constitutes a breaking change for a library whose public surface is Python type annotations and async function signatures.
Any change that breaks existing call sites without code changes
Renaming a parameter, removing a method, changing a return type, dropping a supported Python version
New adapter class, new optional parameter with default, new context manager helper
Retry logic correction, token cost table update, JWKS cache TTL fix, CVE patch
Release candidate for MAJOR bumps only
Published to internal PyPI with the rc label; not installed unless explicitly pinned
Adding a new optional keyword argument with a default is MINOR. Changing a return type from
is MAJOR even if the data is equivalent, because mypy strict mode flags it without a code change.
All bug fixes, security patches, new features. Migration guide published within 30 days of next MAJOR GA.
CVE patches only. No bug fixes unless the bug is a security vulnerability. Scorecard emits a warning.
No patches. Scorecard blocks promotion-to-production for services pinned to an EOL version.
Active support for a MAJOR version ends when the
next MAJOR version has been GA for 12 months
— not at a fixed calendar date. This means v1 stays Active until v2 has been out for a full year, giving teams adequate runway to migrate.
Deprecating a public API symbol follows a mandatory five-step sequence. The platform team must complete all steps before removing a symbol in a MAJOR bump.
At start of Security Only phase
CHANGELOG entry with symbol name, rationale, and replacement
Python warning on every call to the deprecated symbol
Automated Slack DM to each team whose service imports the symbol
Promotion blocked; Backstage badge turns red
Import raises ImportError with migration instructions
Within 48h for CVE; ≤14 days for bugs
Annually or on forced breaking change
hotfix/CVE-XXXX branched from the affected release branch

branch always represents the latest stable state and is always releasable. Release branches are created for each MINOR so that backport patches can be applied without carrying forward unrelated changes from
When a CVE is reported — either against the library itself or a transitive dependency — the platform team follows the triage and patch SLA below.
runs in the library's CI on every push and nightly to catch new advisories automatically.
Blocks promotion to production after 7 days

# .github/workflows/security.yml  — runs on push and nightly at 02:00 UTC
name: Security Audit
on:
  push:
    branches: [main, release/**]
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 2 * * *"

jobs:
  pip-audit:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"
      - run: pip install pip-audit
      - run: pip-audit --requirement requirements.txt --vulnerability-service osv --fail-on-cvss 7.0
      - name: Notify on failure
        if: failure()
        uses: slackapi/slack-github-action@v1
        with:
          channel-id: platform-genai-security
          slack-message: "pip-audit found High/Critical CVE in ${{ github.repository }} on ${{ github.ref }}"
        env:
          SLACK_BOT_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SLACK_BOT_TOKEN }}

Every MAJOR release ships a migration guide within 30 days of GA. The guide follows a fixed structure so that consuming teams and automated tools (like a future LLM-powered upgrade assistant) can parse it consistently.

# Migrating from internal-llm-sdk v1.x to v2.x

## Breaking changes at a glance
| Old symbol | New symbol | Required code change |
|---|---|---|
| `sync_complete(messages, model)` | `await client.complete(req)` | Wrap in async; construct `CompletionRequest` |
| `CompletionResult.text` | `CompletionResponse.content` | Rename field access |
| `LLMProvider.call()` | `LLMProvider.complete()` | Rename method calls |

## Step-by-step upgrade

### 1. Update pyproject.toml
```toml
# Before
internal-llm-sdk = "~=1.8"

# After
internal-llm-sdk = "~=2.0"
```

### 2. Replace sync_complete with async complete
```python
# Before (v1)
from internal_llm_sdk import sync_complete
result = sync_complete(messages, model="gpt-4o")
text = result.text

# After (v2)
from internal_llm_sdk import LLMClient, CompletionRequest, Message
client = LLMClient(build_provider("gpt-4o"), service_name="my-service")
req = CompletionRequest(messages=messages, model="gpt-4o")
resp = await client.complete(req)
text = resp.content
```

### 3. Update result field access
All `.text` accesses must become `.content`.

## Verification checklist
- [ ] `pip install internal-llm-sdk~=2.0` with no resolution errors
- [ ] `mypy --strict` passes
- [ ] Unit tests pass with `MockAdapter`
- [ ] `DeprecationWarning` filter shows no warnings: `python -W error::DeprecationWarning -m pytest`

Before a MAJOR GA, the platform team publishes RC builds to the internal PyPI staging index. Teams are encouraged to test their services against the RC during the 4-week window to surface integration issues before the version is locked.

# Temporarily pin to the RC in a feature branch
[tool.poetry.dependencies]
internal-llm-sdk = "2.0.0rc3"

# Add the staging index (separate from production PyPI)
[[tool.poetry.source]]
name     = "internal-staging"
url      = "https://artifactory.internal/artifactory/api/pypi/genai-staging/simple"
priority = "primary"
Run the full test suite against the RC on your main branch — do not merge RC pins.
If you find a regression, file an issue in the library's internal GitLab repo tagged with the RC version number.
The platform team triages RC feedback within 2 business days and publishes a corrected RC if needed.
After the GA, revert the pin to the compatible-release form (e.g. ~=2.0) and merge normally.

Libraries that skip the RC window and pin to GA on release day have no recourse if a breaking regression slips through. The 4-week window exists precisely to catch issues that the library's own test suite cannot see — integration-specific edge cases in consuming services.

Platform libraries track the CPython support window: a Python version is supported for 5 years after its GA date. The libraries drop Python versions at MAJOR bumps — not MINOR — to avoid forcing consumers onto a new interpreter for a feature update.
CI runs the test matrix against all supported Python versions on every PR. The published wheel sets
so that pip rejects installation on unsupported interpreters with a clear error message rather than a runtime failure.

[tool.poetry.dependencies]
python            = "^3.11"
internal-llm-sdk  = "~=2.4"   # compatible release: >=2.4, <3.0
genai-auth        = "~=1.8"
genai-telemetry   = "~=1.6"
Default — auto-upgrades through patches and minor features, never crosses MAJOR
Use only when a specific minor version introduced behaviour a later minor version broke
Never — locks out all security patches
Never — would auto-upgrade across MAJOR boundaries
Automated Version Inventory and Scorecard Enforcement
A nightly pipeline scans every registered service's
, categorises each library pin against the current support window, and feeds the results into the Backstage scorecard API.
Misses all security patches; manual bump required for every CVE
Deprecated symbol breaks at import in the next MAJOR
Add -W error::DeprecationWarning to pyproject.toml's pytest ini options
Vendoring library source into the repo
Misses all patches; diverges silently from platform standards
Conflicts with the library's own constraint; breaks dep resolution
Open a ticket with the platform team; do not pin transitive deps
Discover breaking regressions only after GA is locked
Run test suite against each RC in a feature branch
Auto-upgrades to the next MAJOR at release; breaks at import
Always set an upper bound with ~ or <

A clear versioning and support policy converts shared platform libraries from an implicit dependency into a managed contract. Semver with explicit breaking-change rules lets consuming teams automate updates safely through compatible-release pins and Renovate. Published support windows — 12 months Active, 6 months Security Only, then EOL with scorecard enforcement — give teams a predictable migration timeline. The mandatory deprecation sequence (announce → warn → notify → block → remove) ensures no symbol disappears without adequate notice. Monthly MINOR releases, a 24-hour hotfix SLA for Critical CVEs, structured migration guides, a 4-week RC feedback window, and automated import-graph scanning provide the operational infrastructure that makes the policy enforceable at scale across dozens of consuming services.
